message:
🐛 fix(affiliate): credit GH-login utm_term under intern_outreach + shorten cache TTL (#9153)

Two changes to affiliate-clicks.mts that address interns reporting stuck
click counts on the contributor leaderboard:

### 1. Recover clicks currently dropped by Query 1

GA4 data on 2026-04-19 showed a row with
`sessionCampaignName=intern_outreach` and `sessionManualTerm=xonas1101`
(not 'intern-06'). The previous Query 1 loop only accepted terms present
in INTERN_MAP and silently skipped everything else, so that click — and
every similar 'mixed campaign + GitHub-login term' share — dropped on the
floor. This happens in practice because mentees are transitioning from
the legacy 'intern-0X' utm_term to using their GitHub handle, sometimes
while still tagging the share with 'intern_outreach' out of habit.

Now Query 1 falls back to treating an unknown utm_term AS the GitHub
login when it matches GitHub's login pattern (^[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9]
|-(?=[a-zA-Z0-9])){1,38}$, 2-39 chars, no consecutive hyphens).

### 2. Tighten Query 2's guard

Query 2 (contributor_affiliate) previously accepted any utm_term as a
GitHub login, which would create phantom rows on '(not set)' or typo
terms. Now gated by isPlausibleGitHubLogin() for consistency.

### 3. Drop cache TTL 15m → 3m

The leaderboard is the primary feedback loop for mentees sharing their
links. A 15-minute function cache compounded GA4's own 24-48h
attribution lag and made the leaderboard feel frozen. 3m is short enough
that a share propagates to the leaderboard within a handful of minutes
(post-GA4-processing) without hammering the Analytics Data API.

GA4's 24-48h attribution-dimension processing window cannot be
shortened from here; a separate leaderboard footnote (docs repo) will
explain that.

### Observed impact

Rishi (intern-01): reported sharing with 50 people, saw 16 clicks.
Ghanshyam (intern-02): reported stuck at 9, actually at 11.
Arnav (intern-03): reported stuck at 9.

The dropped-row fix directly recovers any mixed-shape shares in the past
90 days of lookback data on the next cache refresh (3m).

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
